title: oxalis uspallatensis
intro:
Oxalis uspallatensis, also known as the Patagonian wood sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae) that is native to the Andes mountains in South America.

The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Its leaves and roots contain oxalic acid, which has been shown to have antioxidant, antidiabetic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Oxalis uspallatensis include the potential treatment of various conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and skin infections, and it may also help to lower blood sugar levels and improve immune function. 

Herbal preparations of Oxalis uspallatensis can be made by infusing the leaves and roots in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and grinding them into a powder for use in capsules or tinctures.
